Jake Lovelock · Sussex · Tudor BB58 Winner ★★★★★ Trustpilot 5.0 4 verified winner reviews Blockchain-Verified Draws Cryptographic proof you can verify yourself. Entries are hashed and locked on-chain before the draw — no changes possible — and every result gets a public verification link. We replace "trust us" with "verify it yourself." The Standard — Independent Verification Blockchain-verified draws. Proof you can check yourself. Every draw is cryptographically recorded and given a public proof link. You don't have to take our word for any result — the maths is open for anyone to verify. 01 Blockchain-locked entries Entries are cryptographically hashed and stored on-chain before the draw runs. Once locked, nothing can be added, removed, or changed. 02 Tamper-proof results Blockchain verification means the outcome cannot be altered after the draw completes. The result is mathematically fixed the moment it's recorded. 03 Public verification Every draw gets a public link where anyone can independently verify the blockchain proof — entrant list, winning ticket, and timestamp. The Standard — How We Operate Built for collectors who care about provenance and proof. Most competition sites sell five to ten thousand tickets per draw. Odds vanish. Draw dates get pushed. Customer service disappears after the sale. We built The Calibre Club differently — fixed ticket caps, never-extended draws, every result on a public certificate, and the watch handed over in person where possible. 7 Completed Draws 6 Watches Delivered 4 5★ Trustpilot Reviews 0 Extensions. Ever. Are these draws actually real? Every draw runs live on Facebook, is recorded, and is blockchain-verified — entries are hashed on-chain before the draw and the result gets a public proof link you can check yourself. Winner names, locations, and proof links are published within 24 hours. We're a UK company (Asct Holdings Ltd · Companies House 15776783) with seven completed draws and six delivered watches. What are my real odds? Every draw has a fixed ticket cap . The maximum sell-out odds are printed on every entry pack before you pay. 395 tickets means 1 in 395 max odds — no extensions, no extra tickets sold after cap. Will the draw date change? No extensions. Ever. Draws run on the advertised date and time. The Rolex GMT-Master II 'Batman' draw closes Sunday 21 June, 9:30pm — verified externally. Seven completed draws, seven run on schedule. What if I actually win? You'll hear from us within an hour. Most winners are handed their watch in person . Where we can't meet, watches go by insured tracked courier within 48–72 hours. Every delivery is filmed and published — see for yourself. The Standard — How a Draw Runs How a Calibre Club draw works. Transparent by design. Four steps. No small print. I Choose your draw Pick a competition. See the max odds before you pay — printed on every entry pack. II Enter — paid or free Buy tickets from £37.50, or enter free by post. The free postal route is always available — no purchase necessary. III Draw goes live At the stated time, the draw runs live on Facebook. Recorded. Unedited. Certified by VerifiedDraws. IV Watch arrives Most winners are handed their watch in person. Where we can't meet, insured tracked courier. Authenticated. Boxed. Yours. No purchase necessary. Read the free postal entry route → The Handover Every winner, in person. Seven completed draws. Six delivered watches. Four five-star Trustpilot reviews and one more on the way. We don't ask for them — winners leave them because the experience was what we promised. Comp 004 · 1 May 2026 Jake Lovelock · Sussex · Tudor Black Bay 58 Verify This Draw Open on YouTube The Complete Roll Comp 006 · 29 May 26 Chris Smith Birmingham Tudor Black Bay GMT 'Pepsi' Verified · Delivery in progress Comp 005 · 22 May 26 Gillian Gibson Edinburgh, Scotland Tudor Black Bay 58 Burgundy Verified · Delivery in progress Comp 004 · 1 May 26 Jake Lovelock Sussex Tudor Black Bay 58 Verified · Delivered · 5★ Comp 003 Lynda Birtles Bicester, Oxfordshire Omega × Swatch Mission on Earth Verified · Delivered · 5★ Comp 002 Anthony Nolan London Omega × Swatch Mission to Uranus Verified · Delivered · 5★ Comp 001 Alex Griffith Brecon, Wales Omega × Swatch Moonphase Verified · Delivered · 5★ See All Winners & Proof Trail → The Standard — What Makes This Different Built differently. On purpose. The UK competition market runs on volume — thousands of tickets, vanishing odds, postponed draws, no real proof. We built The Calibre Club against every one of those conventions. 395 Max tickets per draw Industry standard is 5,000–10,000+. Our Rolex Batman caps at 395. The maths is simple. The odds are real. 6/7 Delivered & verified Seven completed draws. Six watches delivered, one in progress. Four five-star Trustpilot reviews. Every result on a public certificate. £0 Free postal entry route Every draw has a no-purchase-necessary route, always. Required by law — surfaced by us. Other sites bury it. 0 Extensions. Ever. Draw dates are fixed. We've never extended a draw. The Rolex GMT-Master II 'Batman' closes Sunday 21 June at 9:30pm — and that's that. The Questions The questions you should be asking. Transparency isn't a feature — it's how we operate. Anything not covered here, ask us directly. Is The Calibre Club a legitimate business? Yes. We're a UK company — Asct Holdings Limited, Companies House registration 15776783, registered in England & Wales. Every draw is independently certified by VerifiedDraws.com , conducted live on Facebook, and the winner is publicly named. Seven completed draws, six delivered watches, four verified five-star reviews on Trustpilot. What are my actual odds of winning? Your max sell-out odds are printed on every entry pack before purchase. The Rolex GMT-Master II 'Batman' draw caps at 395 tickets — 1 in 395 maximum. Buying a 25-ticket pack gives you 1 in 16 max odds. We never sell beyond the cap. Will the draw date change after I enter? No. We've never extended a draw and don't permit it in our operating model. Draws run on the advertised date and time, certified externally. Are the watches genuine, and what condition are they in? Every watch is genuine, sourced from reputable UK channels, and authenticated before draw close. Condition is stated openly on each competition page (e.g. pre-owned excellent, full set with box, papers and warranty). What's described is what arrives. Is this gambling? Do I need to be 18+? Our draws are structured as UK prize draws with a no-purchase-necessary postal entry route . Paid and valid free postal entries are included in the same draw, with equal treatment. You must be 18 or over and a UK resident to enter. Can I take a cash alternative instead of the watch? On most draws, yes — but the cash figure is what we paid for the watch, not what it's worth on the grey market. The watch is almost always the better outcome. Specific cash alternatives are stated on each competition page where available. What happens immediately after I enter? You'll get an email with your ticket number(s) straight away. Before the draw, the full entrant list is published on a third-party VerifiedDraws page, so you can confirm your entry is in. The draw then runs live on Facebook at the advertised time, and the winning ticket is certified externally on a public page you can re-check after the event. What if I actually win? You'll hear from us within an hour. Most winners are handed their watch in person by the founder. Where we can't meet, watches go fully insured by tracked courier, typically arriving within 48–72 hours. Every delivery is filmed and published — see the winners page for proof. Do you publish the winning ticket and entrant list? Yes — every draw is blockchain-verified. Entries are cryptographically hashed and locked on-chain before the draw, then the winning ticket number, full entrant list, and timestamp are published on a public proof page anyone can independently verify. Click "Verify This Draw" on any winner card to check it yourself. Is there any kind of subscription or recurring billing? No. There is no membership, no recurring charge, and no automatic billing. You pay per entry, or you enter free by post. That's the entire offer. What if a competition doesn't sell out? The draw still runs at the advertised date and time, with whoever has entered. Lower sell-through means better odds for entrants. We don't cancel, extend, or roll over draws. No Purchase Necessary Every Calibre Club draw has a free postal route. No purchase necessary. To enter free by post, first create a free Calibre Club account at thecalibreclub.com so we can match your entry and contact you if you win. Then post a postcard or unenclosed letter to Unit A, 82 James Carter Road, Mildenhall Industrial Estate, Suffolk, IP28 7DE, including the email address registered to your account, your full name, and the competition you wish to enter. One free entry per envelope, and it must arrive before the closing date. Free postal entries are treated exactly the same as paid entries in the draw. Read the Free Entry Route → The Dispatch Be first for the next draw. New draws sell fast. Subscribers get exclusive entry deals, first sight of new grails, and an occasional dispatch. Nothing else. No spam. Subscribe Subscribers get 24-hour early access and a first-entry discount — nothing else. Unsubscribe any time.

With regular competitions featuring everything from iconic Swiss classics to standout modern releases, the next opportunity is always closer than you think. competitions.product: WIN A NEO-VINTAGE ROLEX SUBMARINER 14060 W/ BOX & BOOKLETS
competitions.product_info: Home – Rolex Submariner 14060 “No Date” WIN A NEO-VINTAGE ROLEX SUBMARINER 14060 W/ BOX & BOOKLETS Value: £6,500 Duration: 7 Days Age: Circa 1997 Max. Entries: 649 Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 16/06/26 The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic and popular wristwatches of all time , and this, the “no date Sub”, is the purest expression of that. This reference, the 14060, is the perfect marriage of classic vintage styling with modern durability and wearability – the perfect neo-vintage sports watch for all occasions. This U serial example dates to 1997 and is in very good condition , accompanied by its box, booklets, tag, and calendar card. 45.61% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 296 649 45.61% 353 Tickets remaining 353/649 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 2 Days 2 Hours 39 Minutes 1 Seconds ENTER NOW WIN A ROLEX SUBMARINER 14060 "NO DATE" 1997 ENTER NOW 45.61% SOLD 0 Tickets Sold: 296 649 45.61% 353 Tickets remaining 353/649 Tickets Remaining Time Left to End: 2 Days 2 Hours 39 Minutes 1 Seconds Value: £6,500 Duration: 7 Days Age: Circa 1997 Entries: 649 Max. Ticket Price: £20 Live Draw: 16/06/2026 The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic and popular wristwatches of all time , and this, the “no date Sub”, is the purest expression of that. This reference, the 14060, is the perfect marriage of classic vintage styling with modern durability and wearability – the perfect neo-vintage sports watch for all occasions. This U serial example dates to 1997 and is in very good condition , accompanied by its box, booklets, tag, and calendar card. #28 - The Rolex Submariner 14060 "No Date" w/ Box & Booklets 1997 The History— The Example The Rolex Submariner is one of the most iconic wristwatches ever created, and for many enthusiasts, the no-date Submariner is the model in its purest form. Free from the date window and Cyclops magnifier, the reference 14060 offers the clean, symmetrical dial layout that made the Submariner famous. Introduced in 1990, the 14060 occupies a particularly appealing place in the Submariner lineage, combining classic proportions and vintage charm with the durability and practicality expected of a modern Rolex. It remains one of the most wearable sports watches the brand has ever produced. This U serial example dates to 1997 and remains in very good condition . Sitting firmly within the tritium era, it retains the character and warmth that collectors look for in a neo-vintage Rolex, while still being a watch that can be enjoyed every day without compromise. Accompanying the watch are its box, booklets, tag and calendar card , making it a strong and increasingly collectible example of one of Rolex’s most enduring references. Overall, a versatile, understated and highly capable sports watch – the 14060 continues to be one of the smartest entries into vintage Rolex ownership. ENTER NOW The First Competition of its Kind The Vintage Watch Draw is unique. We’re the first competition website dedicated to collector-grade vintage watches, backed by a trusted expert. After the initial idea over five years ago, we’re so excited to now bring this new concept to you. Curated by A Trusted Expert Our competitions are curated by Alex Stevens, a trusted & reputable vintage watch specialist with lifelong experience in the trade. 12 Month Service Warranty Every watch is fully serviced by our watchmaker & comes with a 12 month mechanical warranty, so you can enjoy your watch hassle-free. Collector-Grade Condition Every watch is meticulously curated by Alex, known for selecting only the finest, collector-grade examples in terms of condition & originality. Low & Transparent Odds We don’t flood the draw with tickets. Our competitions are deliberately limited to keep odds low & your chance of winning fair. Guaranteed Draws Every draw ends on the stated date, no matter what. We never extend our competitions, even if we haven't sold all the tickets.
